_comment: FROZEN drain worktree template. The gateway copies this verbatim (Bash cp, not Write) into .claude/worktrees/{id}/.claude/settings.local.json at warm step. Loaded via `claude -p --setting-sources local,project`. The outbound write wall is cwd-default-allow + absence of --add-dir <gateway>, NOT blanket Edit/Write denies (which break inbound via deny-overrides-allow). The allow list runs broad common build/test runners INSIDE the worktree (widening inside the work surface does not widen outbound). The deny list is the destructive-git lane — gateway-exclusive — plus settings authorship; it is the security-load-bearing part and stays frozen. On native Windows there is no OS sandbox, so an indirect write through a Bash subprocess (node -e, a build script) is not contained by this layer — that residual is documented in the skill's parallel-worktrees asset; the defense there is the worktree-boundary anchor (embedded at dispatch) + dispatch-contract clarity.
